data.title: Euro/Dollar trims losses to trade flat on day at $1.1632
Gold rises on Iran war safe-haven bid; firm dollar limits upside
data.description:
March 5 (Reuters) - Gold prices rose on Thursday, lifted by safe-haven demand amid an escalating war in the Middle East, while a stronger dollar and concerns around the U.S. Federal Reserve's monetary policy capped gains. 

Spot gold was up 0.6% at $5,168.43 per ounce, as of 0855 GMT. U.S. gold futures for April delivery were up 0.9% at $5,179.20.

Israel launched a large wave of strikes on Tehran on Thursday, targeting what it said was infrastructure belonging to the Iranian authorities, after Iranian missiles sent millions of Israelis rushing into bomb shelters.

"On the one hand, there may be greater safe-haven demand for gold given the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. On the other hand, the risk of a prolonged period of higher energy prices that takes rate cuts off the table, and adds to the chance of rate hikes, could be capping further gains," said Hamad Hussain, a climate and commodities economist at Capital Economics.   

The U.S. dollar rose about 0.3% after briefly retreating from three-month highs, as the fallout from the war roiled global markets and kept sentiment fragile.

Concerns about energy supply continued to drive up oil prices and stoke inflation fears. 

Gold is considered a hedge against inflation in the long run, but also tends to thrive when interest rates are lower, as it is a non-yielding asset.     

President Donald Trump on Wednesday officially nominated former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh to be the U.S. central bank's next chair. 

U.S. economic activity grew slightly, prices continued to increase and employment levels were stable in recent weeks, the Federal Reserve said on Wednesday in its latest "Beige Book" report. 

Markets expect the Fed to keep rates steady at its next policy meeting on March 18, according to CME Group's FedWatch tool.

Investors are looking out for the weekly U.S. jobless claims data, due later today, and the U.S. employment report for February on Friday for further clues on monetary policy this year.

Spot silver rose 0.5% to $83.80 per ounce. Platinum gained 1.1% to $2,172.20, while palladium lost 0.7% to $1,662.07.

(Reporting by Pablo Sinha in Bengaluru; edititng by Andrei Khalip)

By Pablo Sinha

data.title: U.S. dollar index turns negative on day in choppy trading
SJM Holdings Swings to Loss in 2025
Reckitt sees pressures ahead as Emerging Markets drives strong quarter
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC on Thursday said a weak cold and flu season, and challenging trading in Europe, would hamper performance in the first quarter of this year, after reporting a strong end to 2025.

The Uxbridge, London-based consumer goods firm, which owns products such as Nurofen painkillers, Strepils throat sweets and Dettol antiseptic, said pretax profit jumped 83% to GBP3.84 billion in 2025 from GBP2.10 billion in 2024.

Diluted earnings per share more than doubled to 467.2 pence from 203.2p.

Net revenue rose 0.3% on-year to GBP14.21 billion from GBP14.17 billion, with core like-for-like growth of 5.2%, ahead of its 4% to 5% medium-term target, and driven by very strong growth in Emerging Markets.

In the fourth quarter, core LFL gowth was 5.9%, above 5.3% company compiled consensus, with Emerging Markets up 17%, Europe down 4.5% and North America up 2.5%.

For 2025, Emerging Markets LFL revenue grew 15%, with growth across all regions and all categories, driven by our brand and execution strengths, with North America up 0.2% and Europe down 1.4%.

Europe was held back by a challenging consumer environment and the timing of the cold & flu season.

North America saw improved momentum in the second half of 2025 reflecting strong growth in non-seasonal brands.

"2025 has been a strong year with performance ahead of our expectations," said Chief Executive Kris Licht.

"We have more work to do but our geographic footprint, portfolio of Powerbrands and focused organisational structure have strengthened our ability to deliver sustainable long-term growth. We

look forward with confidence," he added.

Category performance was led by Intimate Wellness with double-digit full-year LFL net revenue growth. Germ Protection reported high-single-digit LFL growth, led by Dettol, now Reckitt's largest Powerbrand by net revenue. Reckitt also saw a strong performance from non-seasonal over-the-counter self-care products, which posted high-single-digit LFL growth.

Mead Johnson Nutrition posted LFL growth 3.8%, with the US returning to more normalised trading.

Reckitt proposed a final dividend of 127.8 pence per share, up from 121.7p a year ago, taking the total payout for 2025 to 212.2p, up 5.0% from 202.1p.

For 2026, Reckitt expects LFL net revenue growth in Core Reckitt in its 4% to 5% medium-term guidance range.

But it expects an impact in the first quarter on its seasonal over-the-counter business from a weaker cold and flu season, while the trading environment in Europe is likely to remain challenging.

Reckitt expects low-single-digit LFL net revenue growth in non-core Mead Johnson Nutrition business in 2026, with a mid-single-digit LFL decline expected in the first quarter of 2026 as "we lap the retailer inventory rebuild in North America" a year ago.

In addition, given performance of the 'Fuel for Growth' programme, Reckitt said it has now has confidence in achieving a fixed cost base "below our initial 19% target by the end of 2027."

Shares in Reckitt Benckiser were down 2.3% at 5,910.00 pence each in London on Thursday. The wider FTSE 100 was up 0.5%.

By Jeremy Cutler, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

DHL Shares Plunge at Opening – Cost Developments in the Spotlight
data.description:
FRANKFURT (dpa-AFX) – Contrary to pre-market expectations, shares of the DHL Group continued their recent downward slide with significant losses in Thursday's Xetra trading. Following the release of annual figures and an outlook marked by geopolitical uncertainty, Deutsche Post shares dropped by as much as six percent due to critical commentary on cost developments, reaching just over €45, their lowest level in three months. Most recently, the decline was still nearly five percent.

Overall, experts expressed satisfaction with what the logistics group reported for both the past and upcoming year. Operating profit met expectations, and the outlook also aligns with market estimates, wrote Bernstein analyst Alex Irving.

Michael Aspinall from investment firm Jefferies likewise sees only minor adjustments to the consensus as necessary, but emphasized that he personally had "more aggressive targets" in mind given the cost expectations for the new year.

Marco Limite of Barclays Bank is also critical of this point. He explained that DHL made more savings in 2025 than initially planned, so only modest cost reductions can be expected for 2026. He believes this could weigh on analyst consensus for the new year./tih/mis

data.title: Mirxes Wins Bid for Gastric Screening Project in China
China ramps up tech race with US, sets lower target for growth
data.description:
STORY: At the opening session of China's annual parliament on Thursday, Premier Li Qiang outlined plans for the world's second-largest economy.

He vowed to boost investment in high-tech industries and scientific innovation, saying they are key to strengthening national security and reducing dependence on foreign technology.

The pledge comes amid rising geopolitical tensions and an intensifying rivalry with the U.S.

Li praised China's ability to withstand Trump's tariff hikes, but said "multilateralism and free trade are under severe threat."

"The achievements of the past year have not come easily. We have faced rare external shocks and challenges, combined with a complex and severe domestic situation marked by intertwined and overlapping dilemmas."

Li then announced a 7% increase in the national defense budget, as well as more funds for research and development.

He acknowledged an "acute" imbalance between strong supply and weak demand, subdued market expectations and ongoing risks from a persistent property sector downturn and high local government debts. 

Those challenges have pushed Beijing to set the economic growth target for the year at 4.5-5%.

That's a slight downgrade from last year's 5%.

Analysts say the weaker growth target signals that Beijing recognizes the multi-year challenge of rebalancing its economy.

China's 15th five-year plan pledges to drive an increase in household consumption as a share of economic output.

It also sets new ambitions in areas where China already dominates.

Despite accounting for 85% of the world's EV charging stations it plans to double their numbers within the next three years.

And in AI, Beijing said it would build vast "hyper-scale" computing clusters powered by cheap, abundant electricity.

data.title: Ukraine seeks to postpone next round of trilateral talks with Russia amid Middle East events, Zelenskiy says
data.description:
March 5 (Reuters) - Ukraine spoke to the United States about the possibility of changing location and postponing the next round of trilateral talks with Russia due to events in the Middle East, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said.

"The next Ukraine-U.S.-Russia trilateral meeting was planned for the period from March 5 to 9, depending on developments around the world. Currently, there is another war in the Middle East," he said on X.

(Reporting by Anna Pruchnicka; editing by Philippa Fletcher)

data.title: Reckitt Benckiser Group plc Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Reckitt Benckiser Group plc reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was GBP 14,205 million compared to GBP 14,169 million a year ago. Net income was GBP 3,182 million compared to GBP 1,426 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 4.707 compared to GBP 2.042 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 4.695 compared to GBP 2.038 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was GBP 4.683 compared to GBP 2.036 a year ago.

Diluted earnings per share was GBP 4.672 compared to GBP 2.032 a year ago.

data.title: Tian Tu Capital Sees Return to Profit in 2025
U.S. Futures Tick Down, European Stocks Mixed
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.4% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.5%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 declined 0.4% in morning trading. Rentokil Initial climbed 9.1% and Davide Campari-Milano rose 7.4%. On the other hand, Nexi lost 13.4%, and Amplifon slipped 11.8%. The FTSE 100 lost 0.3%. Other stocks in Europe were down as France's CAC 40 decreased 0.5% and Germany's DAX fell 0.5%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index rose 0.3% to 95.88.

In commodities, Brent crude gained 3.3% to $84.09 a barrel, and WTI crude gained 3.7% to $77.40 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 10.6% to 53.95 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield climbed by 4 basis points to 2.796% from 2.757%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury rose by 2 basis points to 4.127% from 4.104%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were up as Japan's Nikkei 225 index gained 1.9%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng gained 0.3%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.6%.


-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-05-26 0347ET
